page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.10xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 40 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 9.6 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the ecs.hfr6.10xlarge demonstrates solid memory and compute capabilities. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1436.39 and a multi-core score of 38318.39, while the Passmark CPU score reaches 34031.19. Memory performance is highlighted by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 2128.36 GB/sec and an uncached large block read bandwidth of 111.38 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance handles 32.74 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 14328.81 in Passmark database tests. It also supports light AI workloads, processing 56.33 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B LLM model.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.hfr6.10xlarge is the complete absence of local NVMe storage and GPU hardware, which limits its suitability for heavy local-disk I/O operations or hardware-accelerated deep learning. However, its high memory density relative to CPU resources makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads that require large memory footprints without the overhead of paying for unneeded local storage or GPU silicon. This hardware profile is highly suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.10xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 40 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz. With 384.0 GB of RAM, it delivers a substantial 9.6 GB of memory per core, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong memory performance, with cached read bandwidth reaching 2128.36 GB/sec and Redis SET operations performing at 32.74 million operations per second. The CPU delivers a Passmark score of 34031.19 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 38318.39. This profile makes the ecs.hfr6.10xlarge an efficient option for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring large memory capacities without the added cost of local NVMe or GPU hardware.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.10xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 40 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for in-memory databases and caching.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high memory density for demanding in-memory database workloads.

384.0 GB of RAM providing 9.6 GB per core
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and enterprise applications
